achievements.title: Amina - From Kibera to University
achievements.description: Amina joined KGSA in 2015 as a timid 12-year-old. Through sports and education support, she developed confidence and academic excellence. She is now in her second year at University of Nairobi studying Engineering.
achievements.impact: Transitioned from struggling in school to top marks; now pursuing higher education in a male-dominated field.

achievements.title: 100% Secondary School Completion Rate
achievements.description: In 2024, 100% of KGSA girls completed their secondary school education and passed their final exams. This is significantly higher than the regional average of 62%.
achievements.impact: All 45 girls in the 2024 cohort will transition to higher education or professional training.

achievements.title: Soccer Tournament Champions
achievements.description: KGSA girls' soccer team won the Kibera Youth Sports Championship for the third consecutive year in 2024. The team demonstrated exceptional skill, teamwork, and sportsmanship.
achievements.impact: Builds confidence and creates positive role models for younger girls in the community.

achievements.title: Life Skills Impact Study
achievements.description: Research conducted in 2023 showed that 87% of KGSA girls reported improved financial literacy, 91% showed increased leadership confidence, and 94% had better understanding of health and safety topics.
achievements.impact: Evidence-based demonstration that our holistic approach works.

achievements.title: First Alumni Medical Doctor
achievements.description: Dr. Mary Omondi, KGSA graduate of 2010, completed her medical degree at Kenyatta University in 2023 and is now practicing at Nairobi Hospital. She mentors current KGSA students.
achievements.impact: Inspires current students to pursue ambitious career goals; demonstrates breaking the poverty cycle.

achievements.title: Partnership with International NGO
achievements.description: KGSA established partnership with Global Girls Forum, an international organization supporting girls' education. This brought in additional resources and training for our staff.
achievements.impact: Expanded capacity to serve more girls; access to international best practices.

achievements.title: 95% University Admission Rate
achievements.description: Of the 80 KGSA girls who sat for university entrance exams in 2023, 76 gained admission to top Kenyan universities. 12 received full scholarships.
achievements.impact: Demonstrates academic excellence and opens doors to higher education.

achievements.title: Community Scholarship Program
achievements.description: KGSA alumni and donors established a scholarship fund in 2022 that has supported 30 girls from vulnerable families to continue their education without fear of dropping out.
achievements.impact: Ensures that economic barriers don't prevent girls from staying in school.

achievements.title: Sports Sponsorship Deal
achievements.description: In 2022, a local sports company provided equipment, uniforms, and training resources worth 2 million KES to support our four sports programs.
achievements.impact: Expanded sports offerings and improved quality of athletic training.

achievements.title: 500+ Girls Reached Milestone
achievements.description: KGSA celebrated supporting its 500th girl through our programs in 2022. Each girl's life has been positively impacted through education, sports, and community support.
achievements.impact: Demonstrates the scale and reach of our impact in the Kibera community.
